headline: Switzerland formally joins coalition to repatriate Ukrainian children.
summary: Bern upgraded from observer to full member of the International Coalition for the Return of Ukrainian Children at a Brussels high-level meeting on May 11; coalition now numbers 49 countries.

headline: SWISS airline extends cost-cutting to administrative staff; 140 ground staff already exited.
summary: CEO confirmed plans to reduce admin headcount by ~10% through voluntary departures after an earlier cabin crew buyout.

headline: Vaud submits CHF 200M+ Grand Council request for integrated digital patient record.
summary: The canton's financing request covers a shared system for Lausanne University Hospital (CHUV) and eleven Federation of Vaud hospitals — one of the largest hospital IT investments in Western Switzerland to date.

headline: Real-time GPS monitoring of domestic violence perpetrators to pilot in Vaud (H2 2026).
summary: Switzerland is rolling out electronic bracelets that trigger live alerts if offenders breach court-ordered exclusion perimeters; Vaud will run a 6–12 bracelet pilot in the second half of 2026, with most cantons targeting 2027 full rollout.

headline: Trump's 72-hour Russia–Ukraine Victory Day ceasefire expired at midnight May 12; no prisoner swap occurred.
summary: Both sides accused each other of violations throughout the truce (May 9–11): Ukraine said Russian drones and artillery struck Kharkiv and Kherson, killing at least two civilians; Moscow insisted it "strictly observed" the terms.

headline: US–China trade follow-up: He Lifeng delegation in Seoul for May 12–13 talks.
summary: Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng leads trade negotiators to South Korea to meet Treasury Secretary Bessent and USTR Greer — the first detailed implementation talks since the May 2025 Geneva framework that paused most tariffs for 90 days.

headline: US Navy and Iran exchange fire in Strait of Hormuz; UAE struck for first time since April ceasefire.
summary: US Central Command says it "intercepted unprovoked Iranian attacks" on three Navy destroyers transiting the strait and responded with self-defence strikes, sinking six small Iranian boats; Iran claims the US fired first.

headline: Japan PM Takaichi eyes May 19 summit visit to Seoul.
summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is considering a two-day trip to South Korea for talks with President Lee Jae Myung in Andong; expected agenda covers energy security after Hormuz disruptions, critical mineral supply chains, economic security, and trilateral DPRK/China/US dynamics under renewe

headline: Anesthetized human hippocampus decodes language and predicts upcoming words.
summary: Baylor College of Medicine researchers recorded hippocampal neurons in unconscious patients under general anaesthesia and found them detecting auditory "oddball" tones (with improving accuracy over time, suggesting neural plasticity), distinguishing parts of speech in running stories, and anticipati

headline: Single HPV vaccine dose protects Kenyan women for 4.5 years with 99.3% efficacy.
summary: A randomised cross-over trial in Kenya found a single bivalent HPV dose conferred 99.3% (95% CI: 96.2–99.9%) protection against persistent HPV 16/18 infection at 4.5 years — the strains responsible for most cervical cancers.

headline: Thunderstorm physics far stranger than classical models predict.
summary: New instrument campaigns — including rockets trailing wires to trigger bolts deliberately, and space-based gamma-ray detectors — reveal that storm clouds produce gamma-ray flashes and relativistic electron beams unexplained by standard charge-separation theory.

headline: arXiv:2605.06375
summary: (~May 2026) — *A Unified Pair-GRPO Family: From Implicit to Explicit Preference Constraints for Stable and General RL Alignment.* Proposes a family of pair-based GRPO variants with explicit preference constraints to fix instability, ambiguous gradient directions, and high variance endemic to standar

headline: arXiv:2605.07105
summary: (~May 2026) — *Theoretical Limits of Language Model Alignment.* Formal analysis of the fundamental tradeoff between capability preservation and alignment strength; proves hard limits on how far alignment can shift model behaviour without degrading general capability.

headline: arXiv:2605.07461
summary: (~May 2026) — *Think-with-Rubrics: From External Evaluator to Internal Reasoning Guidance.* Converts externally defined evaluation rubrics into internal chain-of-thought scaffolding, improving reasoning quality on complex tasks without additional supervised training data.

headline: arXiv:2605.06665
summary: (~May 2026) — *UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts.* Replaces per-layer expert pools with a single shared global pool across all MoE layers, reducing redundancy and improving routing efficiency.

headline: arXiv:2605.06609
summary: (~May 2026) — *SoftSAE: Dynamic Top-K Selection for Adaptive Sparse Autoencoders.* Replaces fixed-K sparse autoencoders with learned adaptive gating, yielding sparser and more interpretable feature dictionaries with fewer spurious activations.

headline: Asia closed mixed on Tuesday 12 May.
summary: Hang Seng +0.34% to 26,495; Nikkei 225 +0.20%, Topix +0.54%; KOSPI, NZX 50, and ASX 200 declined.

headline: EUR/CHF 0.9151; EUR/USD ~1.18; implied USD/CHF ~0.775.
summary: ECB reference rate feed blocked from sandbox; figures from May 10 data.

headline: US equity futures: no Tuesday premarket data retrieved.
summary: Sunday-night baseline (10 May): S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures –0.2%, Dow –0.3%.
